block_00: The US Non-Farm Payrolls Report is out Tomorrow – Don’t Miss-out!
block_01: Non-Farm Payrolls: Will the US Economy Collapse?
block_02: This Friday, on 8 May 2020, 12:30 p.m. GMT, the US non-farm payrolls (NFP report) will be released. This report is considered one of the most eagerly-awaited economic events for traders all over the world. Let’s figure out why is it so.
block_04: Non-farm payrolls is an employment report released monthly by the US Bureau of Labor Statistics. It reflects the change in the number of employees engaged in the non-farm sector of the US economy. The report is usually out on the first Friday of each month (except for public holidays) at 12:30 p.m. GMT.
block_06: Simply put, the higher the number of payrolls – the better for the U.S. economy, and vice versa. This is because new jobs contribute to Americans’ solvency improvement, as well as increase the tax flow for the state treasury. All these factors directly affect the value of the United States dollar.
block_07: What to Expect from the April NFP Report?
block_08: The previous non-farm payrolls showed that the US economy lost 701 thousand jobs in March 2020, reaching negative values for the first time in a long time, and that’s not even the hard part.
block_09: According to the April estimates, private payrolls fell by more than 20 million,meaning that over 20 million people in the US have lost their jobs in a one-month period.
